# Review Principles

- Engineering review is the default gate. Skip it only when there is genuinely no implementation impact.
- Add design review when a user will see or feel the change directly.
- Add devex review when another developer must adopt, configure, extend, or upgrade the change.
- CEO review is most useful when scope, ambition, or differentiation is still negotiable.
- Preserve disagreements as explicit decisions instead of averaging them into vague prose.
- The reviewed plan should end with validation commands, rollback notes, and the next concrete action.
